prompt:
The ops console I maintain has a command box where people type things like

    deploy web-1 --note 'rollback of "fridays mess"' # ticket 8123

and today we split it with str.split(). You can imagine how that's going. I know shlex exists; we're not using it — it has posix/non-posix mode surprises, its error messages are useless to surface in a UI, and half its behavior (punctuation_chars? whitespace_split?) is configuration we'd have to pin down anyway. I'd rather own 80 clean lines. So: no shlex, no re, stdlib only.

Build cmdlex.py exposing `tokenize(line) -> list[str]`. Rules, which I've written on the whiteboard and now here:

- spaces/tabs separate tokens; runs collapse; leading/trailing ignored; empty/blank line -> [].
- single quotes: absolutely literal until the closing quote. Backslash has no power inside.
- double quotes: spaces don't split; backslash escapes only \" and \\, in front of anything else the backslash stays in the output as-is.
- backslash outside quotes: escapes whatever comes next (space, quote, #, anything).
- quoting glues: foo'bar'"baz" is ONE token foobarbaz; '' and "" produce a real empty-string argument (we need that to pass empty values to flags).
- comments: an unquoted # begins a comment ONLY where a new token could start; file#1 and "#quoted" keep their #.
- unterminated quote or a dangling backslash at end of line -> ValueError (the console shows it to the user, so raise, never guess).

test_cmdlex.py is the contract — `python3 test_cmdlex.py` clean, no edits to it.
